target.name: Arbe Robotics
target.founder: Kobi Marenko, Noam Arkind, Oz Fixman

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Joby Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $10B+

private.name: Archer Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $5B

data.narrative:
Arbe Robotics is a public-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector that has raised $118M to date. The valuation landscape for this segment remains sparse: the identifiable peer universe comprises six private companies, including Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation, Nuro, Elroy Air, and Aurora Innovation, with zero public comparables available. This absence of liquid public benchmarks and the narrow private peer set—only two same-stage companies identified—constrains traditional valuation methodology.

The Peer-Median approach is not applicable to this analysis. The Drones & Autonomous sector contains only four same-sector peers total, but filtering for stage-alignment, adjacent-stage proximity, or valuation-tier similarity failed to yield the minimum three datapoints required for a defensible median calculation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable methodological noise given the heterogeneity of business models and technology readiness levels across the broader autonomous systems landscape.

Given these constraints, a robust triangulated valuation range cannot be established from the provided data. Forward progress on valuation would require either: (1) expansion of the private peer set through additional fundraising or M&A transactions that establish transaction pricing, or (2) monetization milestones from Arbe or peer companies that enable revenue multiple calibration. The key uncertainty remains the absence of precedent transactions or public market comparables sufficient to anchor valuation bands.
